heading: Definitions
label: 300mm–5 Definitions
notes: Amendments2023—Pars. (5) to (19). added pars. (5) and (13) and redesignated former pars. (5) to (11) and (12) to (17) as (6) to (12) and (14) to (19), respectively.Pub. L. 118–31
amendments: Amendments2023—Pars. (5) to (19). added pars. (5) and (13) and redesignated former pars. (5) to (11) and (12) to (17) as (6) to (12) and (14) to (19), respectively.Pub. L. 118–31

children.text: The term “aggravating” means, with respect to a health condition, a health condition that existed on , and that, as a result of exposure to airborne toxins, any other hazard, or any other adverse condition resulting from the , terrorist attacks, requires medical treatment that is (or will be) in addition to, more frequent than, or of longer duration than the medical treatment that would have been required for such condition in the absence of such exposure.September 11, 2001September 11, 2001
The term “certified-eligible WTC survivor” has the meaning given such term in .section 300mm–31(a)(2) of this title
The terms “Clinical Center of Excellence” and “Data Center” have the meanings given such terms in .section 300mm–4 of this title
The term “enrolled WTC responder” means a WTC responder enrolled under .section 300mm–21(a)(3) of this title
The term “Federal agency” means an agency, office, or other establishment in the executive, legislative, or judicial branch of the Federal Government.

The term “initial health evaluation” includes, with respect to an individual, a medical and exposure history, a physical examination, and additional medical testing as needed to evaluate whether the individual has a WTC-related health condition and is eligible for treatment under the WTC Program.

children.label: A
children.text: for WTC responders, the health conditions listed in ; andsection 300mm–22(a)(3) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: for screening-eligible and certified-eligible WTC survivors, the health conditions listed in .section 300mm–32(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the area of Manhattan that is south of Houston Street; and

children.label: B
children.text: any block in Brooklyn that is wholly or partially contained within a 1.5-mile radius of the former World Trade Center site.

The term “New York metropolitan area” means an area, specified by the WTC Program Administrator, within which WTC responders and eligible WTC screening-eligible survivors who reside in such area are reasonably able to access monitoring and treatment benefits and initial health evaluation benefits under this subchapter through a Clinical Center of Excellence described in subparagraphs (A), (B), or (C) of .section 300mm–4(b)(1) of this title
The term “screening-eligible WTC survivor” has the meaning given such term in .section 300mm–31(a)(1) of this title

Any reference to “” shall be deemed a reference to the period on such date subsequent to the terrorist attacks at the World Trade Center, Shanksville, Pennsylvania, or the Pentagon, as applicable, on such date.September 11, 2001

The term “, terrorist attacks” means the terrorist attacks that occurred on , in New York City, in Shanksville, Pennsylvania, and at the Pentagon, and includes the aftermath of such attacks.September 11, 2001September 11, 2001
The term “uniformed services” has the meaning given the term in .section 101(a) of title 10
The term “WTC Health Program Steering Committee” means such a Steering Committee established under .section 300mm–1(b) of this title
The term “WTC Program” means the Word Trade Center Health Program established under .section 300mm(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: subject to subparagraph (B), with respect to paragraphs (3) and (4) of (relating to enrollment of WTC responders), and the corresponding provisions of (relating to payment for initial health evaluation, monitoring, and treatment, paragraphs (1)(C), (2)(B), and (3) of (relating to determination or certification of screening-eligible or certified-eligible WTC responders), and subpart 3 of part B (relating to payor provisions), an official in the Department of Health and Human Services, to be designated by the Secretary; andsection 300mm–21(a) of this titlesection 300mm–22(c) of this titlesection 300mm–32 of this title1section 300mm–31(a) of this titleSo in original. A closing parenthesis probably should precede the comma.1

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to any other provision of this subchapter, the Director of the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health, or a designee of such Director.

children.label: B
children.text: In no case may the Secretary designate under subparagraph (A)(i) the Director of the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health or a designee of such Director with respect to (relating to payment for initial health evaluation, monitoring, and treatment).section 300mm–32 of this title
The term “WTC-related health condition” is defined in .section 300mm–22(a) of this title
The term “WTC responder” is defined in .section 300mm–21(a) of this title
The term “WTC Scientific/Technical Advisory Committee” means such Committee established under .section 300mm–1(a) of this title
